hitchens_question: When the Pentagon announces a "Cyber Mastery Incentive Pay" program while simultaneously failing to establish a proper Cyber Force and letting Section 702 surveillance authorities lapse, one must ask: are we witnessing the militarization of incompetence, or merely the bureaucratization of our own digital subjugation? The same government that cannot secure its own data centers now promises to wage algorithmic warfare while selling the very drone technology to Taiwan that it fears China might one day turn against us. Tell me, which is more dangerous to a free society—the enemy who seeks to destroy your networks, or the defender who doesn't understand them?
